Infrared vision.<[silence:50ms]> Cosmic time machine.<[silence:50ms]>
The name honors James Webb, NASA’s leader during the Apollo era, as this telescope peers deeper into space and time than any before.<[silence:50ms]>
A mirror 6.5 meters wide, coated in gold, captures faint light from the first galaxies born after the Big Bang.<[silence:50ms]> It orbits the Sun a million miles from Earth, shielded from heat by a sunshield the size of a tennis court.<[silence:50ms]> Three related subjects are dark matter, exoplanet atmospheres and galaxy formation.<[silence:50ms]>
clean_story:
Infrared vision. Cosmic time machine.
The name honors James Webb, NASA’s leader during the Apollo era, as this telescope peers deeper into space and time than any before.
A mirror 6.5 meters wide, coated in gold, captures faint light from the first galaxies born after the Big Bang. It orbits the Sun a million miles from Earth, shielded from heat by a sunshield the size of a tennis court. Three related subjects are dark matter, exoplanet atmospheres and galaxy formation.
